title: permitted_freedom token vocabulary (v1)
description: Structural schema for spec-parity/permitted-freedom-vocab.json — the single source of truth for the closed/versioned/additive set of permitted_freedom tokens (SEMANTICS.md sec 3). Each token carries an id (the string a desired node declares), a rule_kind (the machine-dispatchable handler spec-engine implements), and human-facing description/operational_rule prose. spec-graph validates node `permitted_freedom` entries against tokens[].id; spec-engine dispatches the soundness matcher on tokens[].rule_kind.

rule_kinds.description: The closed set of machine-dispatchable rule kinds. Object keys are the rule_kind ids a token may name. `doc` is a reserved non-rule key.

applies_to_dimension.description: Which parity dimension this rule kind governs.

checker_treatment.description: How the checker must treat a firing/non-firing rule.

tokens.description: The v1 token set. CLOSED for validation (an unknown declared token is rejected) but additive across versions.

id.description: The token string a desired-graph node declares in permitted_freedom.

rule_kind.description: Names exactly one key under rule_kinds. The engine has a handler per rule_kind.

description.description: Human-facing summary of what the token permits.

operational_rule.description: The precise realized-side deviation permitted + how the checker treats it.

why_below_frontier.description: Why this is permitted_freedom (SEMANTICS.md sec 3) rather than a violation or refinement.
